triage_text: 82-year-old White Russian female with a history of CAD, DM II, HTN, and metastatic renal cell carcinoma, recently discharged after a CHF exacerbation requiring non-invasive positive pressure ventilation. She presents with acute shortness of breath and decompensated to acute respiratory failure in the ED. She has a history of multiple hospitalizations for pneumonia, NSTEMI, STEMI, and sCHF exacerbation. Accompanied by her granddaughter, she developed acute respiratory distress at her nursing facility before being transferred to the ED. In the ED, initial vital signs: 160 160/80 35.

criterios_ponto_decisao: Acute respiratory failure requiring immediate intervention
llm_response.justificativa: A paciente apresenta insuficiência respiratória aguda com necessidade de intervenção imediata (classificação ESI-1). A taquipneia (35 irpm) está na zona de perigo para adultos. A presença de comorbidades graves (CAD, DM II, HTN, câncer metastático) e histórico de descompensações cardíacas e respiratórias reforçam a necessidade de ressuscitação imediata.
alertas: Monitorar via aérea e oxigenação
Preparar intubação endotraqueal
Avaliar necessidade de ventilação mecânica
Verificar gasometria arterial
Monitorar sinais de hipoperfusão

triage_text: 58-year-old Black/African American male with a history of multiple myeloma post-autologous stem cell transplant, Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, history of pulmonary embolism and deep vein thrombosis, chronic kidney disease, hepatitis B virus infection, and latent tuberculosis. The patient presented with shortness of breath, confusion, and several syncopal episodes. He had a productive cough, fatigue, malaise, and subjective fevers for approximately one week. He experienced worsening shortness of breath and required a wheelchair at the airport. Upon arrival at the emergency department, he was found to be in atrial fibrillation with a heart rate persistently around 100 bpm.

criterios_ponto_decisao: Dor intensa (dificuldade respiratória que impede mobilidade)
recursos_detalhados: Exame de imagem torácica (RX ou TC)
llm_response.justificativa: O paciente apresenta confusão aguda, síncope múltipla e dificuldade respiratória grave que impede mobilidade. A combinação de sintomas neurológicos (confusão) com sinais de instabilidade cardiovascular (fibrilação atrial) e respiratória (dispneia progressiva) ativa o critério B do ESI. Sua história de múltiplas comorbidades (transplante de medula, insuficiência renal, diabetes) aumenta o risco de complicações graves.
alertas: Avaliar risco de embolia pulmonar dada a história de DVT e sintomas atuais
Investigar possibilidade de infecção (febre, tosse produtiva) com exames de imagem
Monitorar função renal e eletrolitos devido à insuficiência renal crônica
Considerar sepse como causa da confusão e síncope

triage_text: 55-year-old male, white, presented to the emergency department following a motor vehicle collision (MVC). The patient was the unrestrained driver of an SUV that hit a construction wall at 80 mph, followed by a rollover. It is unclear if there was a loss of consciousness (LOC). The patient was pulled out of the vehicle by emergency medical services and brought directly to the emergency department. His injuries include left rib fractures and left hemopneumothorax. Past medical history includes hypertension. No known allergies or adverse drug reactions are reported.

criterios_ponto_decisao: Hemotórax e pneumotórax à esquerda (requer drenagem imediata)
Mecanismo de trauma de alta energia (colisão a 80 mph + capotamento)
llm_response.justificativa: O paciente apresenta hemotórax e pneumotórax à esquerda, condições que requerem intervenção imediata para drenagem e evitar insuficiência respiratória. O mecanismo de trauma de alta energia (colisão a 80 mph + capotamento) sugere risco adicional de lesões internas graves. Ambas as condições atendem ao critério de ESI-1 (intervenção imediata para salvar vida).
alertas: Avaliar possibilidade de perda de consciência não documentada
Realizar tomografia computadorizada para excluir lesões torácicas associadas

Avaliar trauma raquimedular devido ao mecanismo de capotamento

triage_text: 59-year-old white female with a history of asthma, bipolar disorder, metastatic ER+/PR+/HER2- breast cancer on palbociclib/letrozole/denosumab with bone involvement, presents with fever, weakness, and hypoxia. Over the past week, she experienced worsening back, right arm, and right lower extremity pain, prompting her to resume MSContin. She also developed a new non-productive cough and felt short of breath. On the day of presentation, she felt weak, confused, and had chills. She fell and called EMS, who transported her to the ED. Vital signs on arrival: BP 133/58, HR 107, RR 20, SpO2 81%, temperature (T) 102.4° F, Pain 7.

llm_response.justificativa: Classificação ESI-2 devido à confusão mental aguda (critério de alto risco), hipóxia grave (SpO2 81%, zona de perigo), febre e sinais de sepse potencial. A paciente idosa com comorbidades complexas (câncer metastático, bipolar) e histórico de queda requer over-triage para garantir avaliação imediata de possíveis complicações oncologicas, infecções ou embolia pulmonar.
alertas: Avaliar risco de embolia pulmonar em paciente com câncer metastático
Investigar sepse em paciente imunocomprometida

triage_text: 55-year-old White female with a history of bladder cancer, status post radical cystectomy with ileal conduit, currently undergoing chemotherapy. She presented with several days of cloudy urine, flank pain, nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. She was initially evaluated at an urgent care center where she was noted to be hypotensive and subsequently transferred to the emergency department (ED). On arrival in the emergency department (ED) vital signs (VS) were: heart rate (HR) 114, respiratory rate (RR) 18, blood pressure (BP) 83/48, SpO2 98%, temperature (T) 99.5° F, pain 0. Past medical history includes small cell carcinoma of the bladder, hypertriglyceridemia, GERD, hypertension, anxiety, laminectomy, and left carotid endarterectomy following TIA symptoms. Family history includes father with unknown vascular disease and mother with COPD. She arrived by ambulance and has allergies to amoxicillin and sulfa drugs.

llm_response.justificativa: A paciente apresenta hipotensão grave (PA 83/48 mmHg) associada a taquicardia (FC 114 bpm), sinais de hipoperfusão (náusea, vômito, diarreia) e histórico de imunossupressão (quimioterapia). Esses critérios indicam choque descompensado, classificando-a como ESI-1 por necessidade de intervenção imediata.
alertas: Monitorar sinais de sepse (exames de sangue, culturas)
Avaliar obstrução urinária secundária à conduíte ileal
Iniciar reposição volêmica e antibióticos amplo espectro

triage_text: 51-year-old white male presented to the ED with the worst headache of his life. His wife found him at home with altered mental status, confusion, drooling, and urinary incontinence. The patient has a history of hypertension and multiple neurosurgical procedures including diagnostic cerebral angiograms, external ventricular catheters, craniotomies for aneurysm clipping, and ventriculoperitoneal shunt placement. No family history of heart disease was noted.

llm_response.justificativa: Paciente com história de neurocirurgia múltipla apresenta cefaleia mais intensa da vida associada a confusão mental, letargia e incontinência urinária. Esses sintomas sugerem evento neurológico agudo de alto risco (ex.: hemorragia subaracnóidea ou ictus isquêmico). A dor intensa e alteração do nível de consciência ativam o Ponto B do ESI.
alertas: Verificar sinais vitais imediatamente
Avaliar escala de Glasgow para quantificar alteração do nível de consciência
Confirmar data da última angiografia cerebral

Vital signs on arrival: BP 85/58, P 61, RR 24, SpO2 100%, T 97.6° F, Pain 0.

Os sinais vitais estão na zona de perigo (hipotensão) e a condição de idoso com comorbidades (insuficiência cardíaca terminal, fibrilação atrial com trombo) reforça a gravidade.

triage_text: 88-year-old male, White, arrived by ambulance after a ground-level fall. He sustained a right clavicle fracture, right rib fractures, right scapula fracture, compression fractures of T10-T12 (chronic), and L1 bilateral transverse process fractures. The patient has a history of atrial fibrillation and hypertension and is on Warfarin. The fall was unwitnessed, and he was found by family approximately one hour later. EMS found him alert and sitting on the floor against the wall, denying any loss of consciousness. He has allergies to doxycycline and statins. The patient's Vitalsigns level is critical.

criterios_ponto_decisao: Trauma com múltiplas fraturas (clavícula, costela, escápula, vértebras) em idoso ≥ 65 anos
Uso de anticoagulante (Warfarin) + trauma com risco de hemorragia interna
Sinais vitais na zona de perigo (critical level)
recursos_detalhados: Tomografia computadorizada de tórax/abdômen para avaliar hemorragia interna
llm_response.justificativa: Classificação ESI-2 devido a trauma múltiplo em idoso com uso de anticoagulante e sinais vitais na zona de perigo. Aplicado over-triage por comorbidades (fibrilação atrial, hipertensão) e risco elevado de complicações em idoso frágil.
alertas: Monitorar INR e risco de hemorragia

triage_text: 80-year-old white female with a history of prior stroke, atrial fibrillation on Coumadin, and seizures. She was transferred from an outside hospital (OSH) with left-sided weakness, altered mental status, right facial droop, and decreased left grip strength, concerning for stroke. Onset was noted at 4:45 pm. The patient denies headache, chest pain, shortness of breath, visual field deficits, and recent tick or mosquito exposure. She has a history of catheter-associated urinary tract infections. Past medical history includes hypertension, hyperlipidemia, carotid stenosis, sick sinus syndrome status post pacemaker placement, seizure disorder, and strokes. No significant family history of premature coronary artery disease or sudden death, but a history of stroke in her mother and maternal grandmother. VS: BP 207/73, HR 100, RR 16, SpO2 98%.

llm_response.justificativa: Paciente idosa (80 anos) com história de acidente vascular cerebral (AVC) anterior, fibrilação atrial em uso de Coumadin e epilepsia, apresentando sinais focais neurológicos agudos (fraqueza do lado esquerdo, ptose facial direita, alteração do status mental e força reduzida na mão esquerda) compatíveis com novo evento isquêmico. A presença de sintomas sugestivos de AVC ativa o Ponto B do ESI, classificando como ESI-2. A over-triage foi aplicada devido à idade avançada com comorbidades (fibrilação atrial, histórico de AVC) e risco elevado de evolução grave.
alertas: Confirmar tempo exato do início dos sintomas para decisões sobre terapia trombolítica

triage_text: 69-year-old male with a history of prediabetes and smoking presented with chest pain and shortness of breath. The pain radiated to his arms and jaw, initially thought to be heartburn. After the pain worsened, he drove himself to the hospital. Initial EKG showed inferior STEMI. Vital signs on arrival were not recorded, which is a critical omission.

criterios_ponto_decisao: Síndrome coronariana aguda com ECG sugestivo de IAM com supradesnível de ST (STEMI)
llm_response.justificativa: O paciente apresenta dor torácica irradiando para braços e mandíbula com EKG confirmador de STEMI inferior, classificando-se como síndrome coronariana aguda grave que requer intervenção imediata. A ausência de sinais vitais não invalida a classificação ESI-1, pois o diagnóstico de STEMI é critério direto para ressuscitação.

Desidratação grave (urina apenas uma vez em 3 dias)

llm_response.justificativa: Classificação ESI-2 devido a dor torácica em paciente idosa com cardiomiopatia (LVEF 35%) e histórico de bloqueio de ramo esquerdo, associada a hipotensão (PA 92/44 mmHg) sugestiva de choque hipovolêmico. triage_text: 86-year-old Asian female with a history of hypertension, chronic atrial fibrillation, mildly dilated ascending aorta and aortic arch, and valvular heart disease, presented with dyspnea for 1 week. She lives alone and has been intermittently noncompliant with her medications, including furosemide. She reports bilateral lower extremity edema, dyspnea, and lower back pain. Recently returned from a trip, she denied any recent fevers, chills, or productive cough but has a non-productive cough. On arrival, she was in atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response (AF with RVR). VS: BP 148/111, HR 140, RR 24, SpO2 100%, temperature (T) 97.0° F, Pain 8.

criterios_ponto_decisao: Fibrilação atrial com resposta ventricular rápida (AF com RVR)
llm_response.justificativa: Classificada como ESI-2 devido à dor intensa (EVA 8/10) e condição de alto risco (fibrilação atrial com resposta ventricular rápida). Os sinais vitais (FC 140 bpm, FR 24 irpm) estão na zona de perigo, reforçando a necessidade de atendimento imediato. A paciente idosa com comorbidades complexas (hipertensão, valvopatia, não compliance com medicações) justifica a classificação emergente.

The patient arrived via ambulance and has allergies to Codeine, Compazine, and Vicodin. VS: BP 143/80, HR 109, RR 26, SpO2 99%.

Avaliar causa do episódio convulsivo (ex: imagem cerebral, eletroencefalograma)

triage_text: 32-year-old Black/African American male with a history of chronic regional pain syndrome of the right lower extremity, chronic low back pain with spinal nerve stimulator, and depression with prior suicidal ideation. The patient was transferred from an outside hospital after presenting with hematemesis and acute liver failure. He had been self-medicating with high doses of Tylenol and ibuprofen for chronic pain, leading to abdominal pain and vomiting blood. Upon arrival, he had grade 1 encephalopathy and a hematocrit of 19. He reported abdominal pain and thirst. He was constipated and had not had a bowel movement for a week. No fever or chills reported. VS: BP 154/77, HR 90, RR 16, SpO2 99%, temperature (T) 99.0° F, Pain 0.

criterios_ponto_decisao: Hematemese (vômito de sangue) sugere sangramento gastrointestinal ativo
Insuficiência hepática aguda com encefalopatia grau 1
Hematócrito de 19% indica anemia grave (provavelmente por sangramento interno)
Autoadministração de doses elevadas de paracetamol (Tylenol) como causa potencial de hepatite medicamentosa
llm_response.justificativa: O paciente apresenta hematemese (sangramento gastrointestinal ativo), insuficiência hepática aguda com encefalopatia grau 1 e hematócrito de 19% (anemia grave). Esses critérios atendem ao Ponto A do ESI-4, indicando necessidade de intervenção imediata para salvar vida. A autoadministração de paracetamol em doses tóxicas é um fator de risco adicional para falência hepática aguda.
alertas: Monitorar sinais de hipovolemia (pulso, pressão arterial, diurese)
Preparar para endoscopia digestiva superior para controle de sangramento

triage_text: 26-year-old Hispanic/Latino female with a history of MCTD on prednisone and Raynaud's phenomenon. She presents with fever to 104°F, chills, tachycardia, and hypotension. On arrival in the emergency department (ED) vital signs (VS) were: heart rate (HR) 139, respiratory rate (RR) 18, blood pressure (BP) 97/54, SpO2 100%, temperature (T) 104.6° F, pain 7.

llm_response.justificativa: A paciente apresenta sinais de choque séptico (hipotensão 97/54 mmHg, taquicardia 139 bpm, febre 104,6°F) com histórico de UTI não tratada adequadamente. A combinação de hipotensão, taquicardia e febre sugere choque séptico, que requer intervenção imediata para salvar vida, ativando o Ponto A do ESI.

triage_text: 55-year-old white male with a history of end-stage renal disease (ESRD) on peritoneal dialysis (PD), who presented with abdominal pain and low-grade fever. The patient has a significant medical history including multiple exit site infections, hyperparathyroidism, vascular calcification, osteoporosis, and a history of Kaposi's sarcoma. The patient walked into the emergency department and reported abdominal pain and a low-grade fever starting that afternoon. He noted that his hernia was protruding more than usual and was tender, with additional tenderness in the left lower quadrant (LLQ) and epigastrium. He did not experience nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, or changes in bowel movements but did have flatus. The patient was concerned about peritonitis due to the cloudy appearance of his PD fluid. His blood pressure is chronically low, with a baseline in the 80s. Initial vital signs in the ED were BP 68/27, HR 106, RR 16, SpO2 97%, and temperature 98.4°F. Upon arrival, he was pleasant and in no acute distress (NAD), complaining only of abdominal pain.

llm_response.justificativa: O paciente apresenta hipotensão severa (68/27 mmHg) associada a febre, dor abdominal, hérnia protrusiva e líquido de diálise peritoneal turvo, sugestivo de peritonite. A hipotensão em paciente com ESRD e sinais de infecção grave (peritonite) configura choque séptico, classificando-se como ESI-1 por risco de vida imediato.
alertas: Monitorar sinais de hipoperfusão (oligúria, palidez, confusão)
Realizar exames de líquido peritoneal (cultura, células)
Iniciar antibioticoterapia empírica imediata

criterios_ponto_decisao: Frequência cardíaca na zona de perigo (>100 bpm)
Necessidade de múltiplos recursos (exames laboratoriais, imagem, endoscopia)

A necessidade de múltiplos recursos (exames laboratoriais, imagem e endoscopia) e os sinais vitais na zona de perigo indicam risco de sangramento ativo grave, classificando-o como ESI-2.

Avaliar necessidade de transfusão sanguínea
Confirmar diagnóstico diferencial com endoscopia

triage_text: 65-year-old white female with a history of multiple sclerosis, dementia, neurogenic bladder with indwelling foley, right staghorn calculus, and left obstructing UVJ stone with nephrostomy tube. Presented with worsening lethargy and no output from nephrostomy tube for two days. Reported to have been satting at 77% on NBR when EMS arrived. Admitted three times in the past year with urosepsis. In the ER, febrile to 102.8, tachycardic, leukocytosis to 16.4. Numerous excoriations within and around her vagina and decubitus ulcers on her sacrum. Nephrostomy tube encrusted with purulent discharge. Erythema and fluctuance with expressible pus around the nephrostomy site. Arrival by ambulance. Allergies: Zosyn, ceftriaxone, tuberculin ppd skin test. Social history: lives in a nursing home. Family history: no family members with multiple sclerosis.
